places.name: Grafali's Coffee Roasters
places.description: Popular campus cafe. Long queues but worth it. Best flat white on campus.
places.insiderTip: Go before 9am to skip the line. The crispy chicken wrap is a hidden gem.

places.name: Church of Secular Coffee
places.description: Cozy, warm atmosphere. Great pastries. Quieter than Grafali's.
places.insiderTip: The staff know all the regulars. Modern comfortable vibe. Open until 8pm.

places.name: Sammy's Cafe
places.description: In the Monash Sport building. Huge portions, locally sourced. Marko's second home.
places.insiderTip: Open 7 days. The catering is surprisingly good for events.

places.name: Monash Wholefoods Co-op
places.description: Student-run co-op. Cheap salads, vegetarian-heavy, good vibes.
places.insiderTip: Best value meal on campus. Rosa approves of their vegetable game.

places.name: Guzman y Gomez (GYG)
places.description: Just opened. Burritos, bowls. Open for breakfast, lunch, dinner.
places.insiderTip: Rosa says it's 'not real Mexican' but students love it. Burrito bowls are best value.

places.name: Halls Cafe
places.description: Dining hub for residential students. Affordable, nutritious meals. Pool table and piano inside.
places.insiderTip: Open to all students, not just residents. Lunch 12-2:30pm, dinner 6-8pm. Student M-Pass gets discounts.

places.name: Sir Louis Matheson Library
places.description: Main campus library. Multiple floors, quiet zones, group rooms.
places.insiderTip: 4th floor window desks are the best-kept secret. Almost nobody goes up there. 24/7 during exam period.

places.name: Learning and Teaching Building
places.description: Modern building with lecture theatres, study spaces, and group rooms.
places.insiderTip: The UNIHACK hackathon is being held here. Great power outlets everywhere.

places.name: Monash Sport
places.description: Full gym, pool, courts, group fitness. Student memberships available.
places.insiderTip: Marko's 6am boot camp is free for first-timers. Doug Ellis Pool is surprisingly good.

places.name: Clayton Campus Bus Interchange
places.description: Main bus hub. Routes 601, 630, 900 and more. Connects to Clayton station.
places.insiderTip: The 601 to Huntingdale station is fastest for the train line. 630 goes through Chadstone.

places.name: The Rainforest Walk
places.description: Tree-lined walking path between science buildings. Surprisingly beautiful for a university.
places.insiderTip: Best at sunset. Doug is usually somewhere along here. Look for his handwritten notes on benches.

places.name: Alexander Theatre
places.description: Campus theatre. Student productions, events, occasional film screenings.
places.insiderTip: They show art-house films sometimes. Check the MSA events calendar.

secretKnowledge: The 4th floor of the Matheson Library has a row of window desks almost nobody uses. Best study spot on campus.
The vending machine in Engineering 31 basement is consistently 50c cheaper than the ones in Campus Centre.
There's a shortcut through the Biological Sciences building that saves 5 minutes getting from Rainforest Walk to College Walk.
The Religious Centre (Building 9) has a meditation room open to everyone, not just religious students. Quietest place on campus.
If you need to print and the Campus Centre printers are full, the ones in the Engineering postgrad room are almost always free after 5pm.
The Doug Ellis Swimming Pool does casual swims for students. Way less crowded than the gym.
Grafali's does a loyalty card — 10th coffee free. But you have to ask for it, they don't advertise it.
The bus interchange has phone charging points on the benches. Most people don't notice them.
During exam period, the library vending machines get restocked twice as often. The chocolate row still runs out by Wednesday.
The rooftop of the Learning and Teaching Building has a study area with city views. You need a student card to access it.
